items.title: Xi takes Trump on tour of Communist Party's seat of power as China visit ends
items.summary: Trump and Xi toured Zhongnanhai, the heavily guarded compound where China's top leaders live and work.

items.title: CIA chief visits Cuba as energy crisis worsens
items.summary: The reported visit to Havana came after the US renewed an offer of aid to ease the effects of its oil blockade.

items.title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
items.summary: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported.

items.title: Five Italians die during cave scuba dive in Maldives
items.summary: Four of the Italians were part of a team from the University of Genoa.

items.title: India's Adanis agree to pay $18m to settle civil fraud case in the US
items.summary: The US securities regulator had accused the Adanis of paying bribes and misleading investors, which they denied.

items.title: US Supreme Court restores abortion pill access for now
items.summary: Abortion pills are the most common method of terminating pregnancies in the US.

items.title: Giant new dinosaur identified from fossils in Thailand
items.summary: The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia and weighs as much as nine elephants.

items.title: 'Pretty miraculous' - 11 rescued after pilot ditches plane in Atlantic
items.summary: The survivors spent about five hours on a life raft before being rescued by the US Air Force off Florida's coast.

items.title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble of Kyiv flats after massive Russian strikes
items.summary: Two children are among at least 16 killed in Ukraine's capital in massive Russian drone and missile attacks, officials say.

items.title: 'Floating armoury' ship reportedly seized by Iran
items.summary: A vessel said to be a “floating armoury” in the Gulf of Oman has been seized by Iranian military personnel, according to reports.

items.title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
items.summary: As El Niño develops this year, scientists are increasingly confident it could be one of the strongest on record with global consequences, as Simon King explains.

items.title: Latvian PM resigns after row over stray Ukrainian drones
items.summary: Drones bound for Russia crashed down in Latvia last week, prompting a political fallout.

items.title: War criminal Ratko Mladić's jail release request denied
items.summary: A UN judge admits the 84-year-old is "in the final stages of his life", but says prison conditions in The Hague ensure his maximum comfort.

items.title: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
items.summary: Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman was convicted last week - but the authorities do not know his whereabouts.

items.title: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
items.summary: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

items.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
items.summary: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

items.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22 people, health ministry says
items.summary: Eight children are among those reported dead after a series of air strikes hit the south of Beirut.

items.title: Brutal raid on woman's birthday party highlights rise of Russian vigilante group
items.summary:
The Russkaya Obshina group stages raids to look for activities contravening
"traditional Russian values".

items.title: Trump brought top CEOs to Beijing but few big deals emerge
items.summary: There were plenty of choreographed ceremonies but no sweeping trade breakthroughs.

items.title: BBC on the ground during march through Jerusalem's Old City
items.summary: Tens of thousands of Israeli celebrants have marched through the area captured by Israel in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war.

items.title: Hezbollah support endures in south Lebanon as ceasefire fails to stop war with Israel
items.summary: Despite continuing Israeli attacks and occupation, many people in the south still believe the armed group is the only force capable of defending them.

items.title: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
items.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

items.title: Warning of record global temperatures
items.summary: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows

items.title: Hundreds of illegal motorbikes bulldozed in New York City crime crackdown
items.summary: A moped had been used in the deadly stray-bullet shooting of a 7-month-old in Brooklyn last month, officials said.

items.title: The Kabul rehab centre hit by deadly Pakistani strike
items.summary: Pakistan says it hit 'military and terrorist infrastructure' - but the UN and victims' families reject this claim.

items.title: 'Total lack of respect': Macron interrupts speaker to ask for silence
items.summary: The French president stood up during a conference in Kenya to tell the audience to quieten down, saying it was “impossible” for speakers to be heard.

items.title: Watch: Soldier on parachuting to remote island to bring Hantavirus aid
items.summary: A specialist team helped supply critical medical support to Tristan da Cunha in the South Atlantic.

items.title: BBC tests out England and Argentina's World Cup training grounds in Kansas City
items.summary: Will Grant visited one of the pitches where England captain Harry Kane and his team will call home ahead of the tournament.

items.title: BBC speaks with civilians inside Iran struggling with impact of war
items.summary: Iranian authorities have imposed an internet ban but using trusted sources, special correspondent Fergal Keane hears from those traumatised by conflict and government repression.

items.title: Rosenberg: Scaled-back Victory Parade in Moscow's Red Square
items.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from Moscow, where the annual Victory Day display was quieter than usual.
